2→ 3→ 4→ 5→# MemoryTree Workflow 6→ 7→**Persistent, Git-tracked project memory for AI coding assistants.** 8→ 9→[![License: MIT](https://img.shields.io/badge/License-MIT-blue.svg)](LICENSE) 10→[![Python 3.11+](https://img.shields.io/badge/Python-3.11%2B-3776AB.svg?logo=python&logoColor=white)](https://www.python.org/) 11→[![Claude Code](https://img.shields.io/badge/Claude_Code-Skill-cc785c.svg?logo=anthropic&logoColor=white)](https://claude.ai/claude-code) 12→[![Platform](https://img.shields.io/badge/Platform-macOS%20%7C%20Linux%20%7C%20Windows-lightgrey.svg)](#requirements) 13→ 14→

15→ Install  •  16→ Usage  •  17→ Features  •  18→ Heartbeat  •  19→ Scripts  •  20→ Docs 21→

22→ 23→
24→ 25→--- 26→ 27→## What It Does 28→ 29→MemoryTree gives AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI) a structured memory layer that survives across sessions: 30→ 31→| | Feature | Description | 32→|---|---------|-------------| 33→| :dart: | **Goals** | Track your project's north star across sessions | 34→| :white_check_mark: | **Todos** | Version-bound task lists that stay in sync with goals | 35→| :speech_balloon: | **Chat Logs** | Append-only session records for full traceability | 36→| :books: | **Knowledge** | Durable notes, specs, and architecture decisions (also stored in goals when scope-relevant) | 37→| :inbox_tray: | **Transcripts** | Import and archive AI chat history from Codex, Claude Code, and Gemini CLI | 38→ 39→All stored as plain Markdown files under `Memory/`, tracked by Git, minimal dependencies. 40→ 41→## Install 42→ 43→```bash 44→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow 45→``` 46→ 47→On Windows (Git Bash): 48→```bash 49→git clone https://github.com/beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow "$USERPROFILE/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow" 50→``` 51→ 52→## Usage 53→ 54→In any Claude Code session, run: 55→ 56→``` 57→/memorytree-workflow 58→``` 59→ 60→The skill will immediately: 61→1. **Detect** — check if the current repo has MemoryTree installed 62→2. **Alert** — display pending notifications from `~/.memorytree/alerts.json` 63→3. **Init** — if missing, scaffold `AGENTS.md` and `Memory/` folders 64→4. **Upgrade** — if partial, add missing pieces without overwriting existing policy 65→5. **Maintain** — if installed, read the active goal/todo and report current state 66→ 67→No manual confirmation needed — the skill acts on the detection result immediately. 68→ 69→## Architecture 70→ 71→### Per-Repository Structure 72→ 73→After initialization, your repo will have: 74→ 75→``` 76→AGENTS.md # AI behavior rules for this repo 77→Memory/ 78→ 01_goals/ # Versioned project goals 79→ 02_todos/ # Task lists bound to goals 80→ 03_chat_logs/ # Append-only session logs 81→ 04_knowledge/ # (optional) Durable project notes 82→ 05_archive/ # (optional) Retired goals/todos 83→ 06_transcripts/ # (optional) AI chat history archive 84→ clean/ # Cleaned Markdown indexes 85→ manifests/ # Import metadata 86→ raw/ # Raw transcript mirrors 87→``` 88→ 89→### Global Directory (`~/.memorytree/`) 90→ 91→Cross-project shared state: 92→ 93→``` 94→~/.memorytree/ 95→ config.toml # User-level settings 96→ alerts.json # Pending notifications 97→ heartbeat.lock # Single-instance lock 98→ logs/ # Heartbeat execution logs 99→ transcripts/ # Global transcript archive 100→ raw/ # By client/project/year/month 101→ clean/ # Cleaned Markdown 102→ index/ 103→ sessions.jsonl # Session metadata 104→ search.sqlite # Full-text search index 105→``` 106→ 107→For the full schema see [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). 108→ 109→## Features 110→ 111→### Memory Management 112→ 113→| Memory Type | Behavior | 114→|-------------|----------| 115→| **Goals** | Changed only after explicit user confirmation of a scope or requirement change. Versioned. | 116→| **Todos** | Kept in sync with the active goal. Updated when progress changes or next task becomes clearer. | 117→| **Chat Logs** | Append-only. Never rewritten or deleted. | 118→| **Knowledge** | Stores architecture decisions, product ideas, operating constraints. Goals may also hold scope-relevant items. | 119→| **Archive** | Stale context moved here or to knowledge files to keep active files concise. | 120→ 121→### Multi-Client Transcript Import 122→ 123→Import AI chat history from three clients: 124→ 125→| Client | Source | 126→|--------|--------| 127→| **Codex** | `~/.codex/sessions/**/rollout-*.jsonl` | 128→| **Claude Code** | `transcript_path` if exposed; otherwise `~/.claude/projects//*.jsonl` | 129→| **Gemini CLI** | Saved chats first; checkpoints under `~/.gemini/tmp//checkpoints` if enabled | 130→ 131→Key principles: 132→ 133→- Raw transcripts preserved unchanged as source of truth 134→- Cleaned Markdown generated by **deterministic code** (not model tokens) 135→- Current repo only stores transcripts belonging to this project; others go to global archive only 136→- First import asks once whether raw transcripts may be committed to the repo 137→ 138→### Cross-Project Search 139→ 140→When searching all memories or chat history: 141→ 142→1. Query `search.sqlite` for fast full-text lookup 143→2. Use `sessions.jsonl` for session-level metadata filtering (client, project, date range) 144→3. Load matching cleaned Markdown for context; confirm exact wording against raw transcripts 145→4. Combine with the current repo's `Memory/` files 146→5. Fall back to scanning `clean/` directory if the index does not exist 147→ 148→### Session Continuity 149→ 150→When you open a new session and want to continue where you left off, ask MemoryTree to show your most recent conversation: 151→ 152→> "Look at my last chat" / "看看我最近的聊天" / "Continue where I left off" 153→ 154→The skill will: 155→ 156→1. **Instant sync** — immediately scan all three client directories (not waiting for the scheduled heartbeat) 157→2. **Locate** — find the latest session for the current project across Claude Code, Codex, and Gemini CLI 158→3. **Summarize** — generate a continuation summary: what was discussed, what was tried, and what remains unresolved 159→4. **Handoff** — you can say "continue" and pick up exactly where the previous session stopped 160→ 161→This works **cross-client**: a Codex session can be recovered from Claude Code, and vice versa. 162→ 163→For details see [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) and [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md). 164→ 165→### Git Safety 166→ 167→MemoryTree follows strict isolation rules to never interfere with your repo's workflow: 168→ 169→| Rule | Description | 170→|------|-------------| 171→| File isolation | Only stages/commits `Memory/**` (excluding `raw/` until approved) and `AGENTS.md` (when managed by MemoryTree) | 172→| Branch isolation | Uses dedicated branch `memorytree/--` | 173→| Commit format | `memorytree(): ` or repo-compatible equivalent | 174→| PR isolation | Separate PR for MemoryTree-only changes | 175→| Auto-merge | Only when repo still enforces its review and CI checks | 176→| Auto-push | On by default; skips if no remote; fast-forward only | 177→| Protected branches | Never overrides branch protection or CI rules | 178→ 179→The skill stops and asks before proceeding in 8 scenarios: mixed product code, AGENTS.md conflicts, unclear target branch, repo forbids bot pushes, CI conflicts, unknown raw transcript permission, cross-project transcripts, or protected branch refusal. 180→ 181→For the full policy see [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md). 182→ 183→### Sensitive Info Scanning 184→ 185→During transcript cleaning, the heartbeat scans for API keys, passwords, tokens, and other secrets: 186→ 187→- Matches are logged as warnings and written to `alerts.json` 188→- **No automatic deletion or redaction** — the user decides how to handle them 189→ 190→### Locale Support 191→ 192→| Feature | Description | 193→|---------|-------------| 194→| Templates | English (`en`) and Simplified Chinese (`zh-cn`) | 195→| Auto-detect | Detects language from repo content first, then system locale | 196→| Reply language | Follows user explicit request > message language > repo locale > template locale > `en` | 197→| Non-destructive | Never rewrites existing files just to translate them | 198→ 199→### Environment Adaptation 200→ 201→MemoryTree does not assume the target repo has a Python environment: 202→ 203→``` 204→Prefer uv run python → fall back to system python → fall back to direct template scaffolding 205→``` 206→ 207→Never auto-creates `.venv` or `pyproject.toml` inside the project. 208→ 209→## Background Heartbeat 210→ 211→The heartbeat automates transcript discovery, import, cleaning, commit, and push without consuming model tokens or requiring human intervention. 212→ 213→**Execution flow**: 214→ 215→``` 216→Acquire lock → Load config.toml → Iterate projects → Discover → Import 217→→ Clean → Commit → (auto_push) Push → Release lock → Exit 218→``` 219→ 220→### Install the daemon 221→ 222→```bash 223→memorytree-daemon install 224→``` 225→ 226→### CLI commands 227→ 228→| Command | Description | 229→|-----------------------------|-------------| 230→| `memorytree-daemon install` | Register the heartbeat with the OS scheduler (cron / launchd / Task Scheduler). | 231→| `memorytree-daemon uninstall` | Remove the scheduled heartbeat task. | 232→| `memorytree-daemon run-once` | Run a single heartbeat cycle immediately. | 233→| `memorytree-daemon watch` | Continuous loop for development and debugging only. | 234→| `memorytree-daemon status` | Show registration state and last execution result. | 235→ 236→### Key settings (`~/.memorytree/config.toml`) 237→ 238→| Setting | Default | Description | 239→|----------------------|----------|-------------| 240→| `auto_push` | `true` | Push to remote after each commit. Skips if no remote is configured. | 241→| `heartbeat_interval` | `"5m"` | Interval between heartbeat executions. | 242→| `log_level` | `"info"` | Log verbosity (`debug`, `info`, `warn`, `error`). | 243→ 244→### Design constraints 245→ 246→- Single execution, stateless, idempotent — safe to re-run at any interval 247→- One computer = one scheduled heartbeat task 248→- Will not silently register — always confirms before `install` 249→ 250→For details see [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) and [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md). 251→ 252→## Design Principles 253→ 254→| Principle | How | 255→|-----------|-----| 256→| **Zero intrusion** | Never changes the repo's branch model, CI, or review process | 257→| **Minimal dependencies** | Scripts target Python standard library; no third-party packages required at install time | 258→| **Deterministic** | Transcript cleaning done by code, not model tokens | 259→| **Single source of truth** | Each concept defined in one reference file, others cross-reference | 260→| **Spec-driven** | Reference docs define the contract; code implements it faithfully | 261→| **User asset** | Memory belongs to the user; auto_push on by default to prevent local data loss | 262→ 263→## Scripts 264→ 265→All scripts live under `scripts/` and target Python 3.11+ stdlib only (no third-party packages). 266→ 267→### Entry Points 268→ 269→| Script | Purpose | 270→|--------|---------| 271→| [`heartbeat.py`](scripts/heartbeat.py) | Background heartbeat — discover, import, clean, commit, push transcripts | 272→| [`memorytree_daemon.py`](scripts/memorytree_daemon.py) | Daemon lifecycle manager: `install` / `uninstall` / `run-once` / `watch` / `status` | 273→| [`recall-session.py`](scripts/recall-session.py) | On-demand transcript sync and latest session recall for cross-session context recovery | 274→| [`init-memorytree.py`](scripts/init-memorytree.py) | Scaffold `Memory/` and `AGENTS.md` for a new repository | 275→| [`upgrade-memorytree.py`](scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py) | Add missing MemoryTree pieces to a partial repository | 276→| [`import-transcripts.py`](scripts/import-transcripts.py) | Import one transcript into repo mirror and global archive | 277→| [`discover-transcripts.py`](scripts/discover-transcripts.py) | Scan local client stores and mirror matching transcripts | 278→| [`detect-memorytree-locale.py`](scripts/detect-memorytree-locale.py) | Print the effective locale for a target repository | 279→ 280→### Internal Modules 281→ 282→| Module | Purpose | 283→|--------|---------| 284→| [`_transcript_utils.py`](scripts/_transcript_utils.py) | Transcript parsing (Codex/Claude/Gemini), import, SQLite indexing, discovery | 285→| [`_config_utils.py`](scripts/_config_utils.py) | Load/validate `~/.memorytree/config.toml` with `tomllib` | 286→| [`_alert_utils.py`](scripts/_alert_utils.py) | Manage `alerts.json` — dedup, threshold-based alerting, failure counts | 287→| [`_lock_utils.py`](scripts/_lock_utils.py) | PID-based lock file with atomic creation and stale lock detection | 288→| [`_log_utils.py`](scripts/_log_utils.py) | Date-rotated file logging to `~/.memorytree/logs/` | 289→| [`_scaffold_utils.py`](scripts/_scaffold_utils.py) | Template rendering and directory scaffolding helpers | 290→| [`_locale_utils.py`](scripts/_locale_utils.py) | Locale detection and normalization | 291→ 292→## Reference Docs 293→ 294→| File | Purpose | 295→|------|---------| 296→| [`references/project-detection.md`](references/project-detection.md) | Detect install state | 297→| [`references/memory-layout.md`](references/memory-layout.md) | Folder and file naming rules | 298→| [`references/update-rules.md`](references/update-rules.md) | When to version goals, todos, and session logs | 299→| [`references/git-policy.md`](references/git-policy.md) | Git defaults, auto-push, and policy merge | 300→| [`references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`](references/heartbeat-scheduling.md) | Heartbeat architecture, daemon CLI, execution flow | 301→| [`references/global-configuration.md`](references/global-configuration.md) | `~/.memorytree/` layout, `config.toml` schema, `alerts.json` format | 302→| [`references/transcript-archive.md`](references/transcript-archive.md) | Transcript archival, cleaning, search, and upload rules | 303→| [`references/locale-selection.md`](references/locale-selection.md) | Locale choice rules | 304→| [`references/response-language.md`](references/response-language.md) | Reply-language precedence | 305→| [`references/execution-environment.md`](references/execution-environment.md) | How to run scripts without a project-local Python environment | 306→| [`references/upgrade-path.md`](references/upgrade-path.md) | Safe adoption flow for partial repos | 307→ 308→## Update 309→ 310→```bash 311→cd ~/.claude/skills/memorytree-workflow && git pull 312→``` 313→ 314→## Requirements 315→ 316→- [Claude Code](https://claude.ai/claude-code) CLI 317→- Python 3.11+ (for transcript and heartbeat scripts; `tomllib` is stdlib in 3.11+) 318→- Git 319→ 320→## License 321→ 322→MIT 323→

1→"""Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog."""
     2→
     3→from __future__ import annotations
     4→
     5→import sqlite3
     6→from pathlib import Path
     7→from typing import Any
     8→
     9→
    10→def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    11→    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    12→    try:
    13→        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
    14→        conn.execute(
    15→            """
    16→            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
    17→                client TEXT NOT NULL,
    18→                project TEXT NOT NULL,
    19→                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
    20→                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
    21→                title TEXT NOT NULL,
    22→                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    23→                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    24→                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
    25→                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
    26→                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    27→                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
    28→                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    29→                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    30→                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    31→                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    32→                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
    33→                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
    34→                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
    35→                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
    36→            )
    37→            """
    38→        )
    39→        conn.execute(
    40→            """
    41→            INSERT INTO transcripts (
    42→                client,
    43→                project,
    44→                session_id,
    45→                raw_sha256,
    46→                title,
    47→                started_at,
    48→                imported_at,
    49→                cwd,
    50→                branch,
    51→                raw_source_path,
    52→                raw_upload_permission,
    53→                global_raw_path,
    54→                global_clean_path,
    55→                repo_raw_path,
    56→                repo_clean_path,
    57→                repo_manifest_path,
    58→                message_count,
    59→                tool_event_count
    60→            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
    61→            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
    62→                title = excluded.title,
    63→                started_at = excluded.started_at,
    64→                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
    65→                cwd = excluded.cwd,
    66→                branch = excluded.branch,
    67→                raw_source_path = excluded.raw_source_path,
    68→                raw_upload_permission = excluded.raw_upload_permission,
    69→                global_raw_path = excluded.global_raw_path,
    70→                global_clean_path = excluded.global_clean_path,
    71→                repo_raw_path = excluded.repo_raw_path,
    72→                repo_clean_path = excluded.repo_clean_path,
    73→                repo_manifest_path = excluded.repo_manifest_path,
    74→                message_count = excluded.message_count,
    75→                tool_event_count = excluded.tool_event_count
    76→            """,
    77→            (
    78→                manifest["client"],
    79→                manifest["project"],
    80→                manifest["session_id"],
    81→                manifest["raw_sha256"],
    82→                manifest["title"],
    83→                manifest["started_at"],
    84→                manifest["imported_at"],
    85→                manifest["cwd"],
    86→                manifest["branch"],
    87→                manifest["raw_source_path"],
    88→                manifest["raw_upload_permission"],
    89→                manifest["global_raw_path"],
    90→                manifest["global_clean_path"],
    91→                manifest["repo_raw_path"],
    92→                manifest["repo_clean_path"],
    93→                manifest["repo_manifest_path"],
    94→                manifest["message_count"],
    95→                manifest["tool_event_count"],
    96→            ),
    97→        )
    98→        conn.commit()
    99→    finally:
   100→        conn.close()
   101→

events.text:
I now have a thorough understanding of the entire codebase. Let me compile the detailed implementation plan.

---

## Implementation Plan: Task A (CI + Integration Tests) and Task B (P0 Heartbeat Daemon)

### Current State Summary

**Codebase inventory:**
- 8 entry-point scripts in `scripts/` (hyphenated filenames)
- 10 internal modules in `scripts/` (underscore-prefixed)
- 4 test files in `tests/` totaling 156 tests, all passing
- No `.github/` directory, no CI configuration, no `pyproject.toml`
- Tests run via `uv run python tests/test_*.py` (each file does `sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_DIR))` internally)
- P0 heartbeat already has substantial scaffolding: `heartbeat.py`, `memorytree_daemon.py`, `_config_utils.py`, `_lock_utils.py`, `_alert_utils.py`, `_log_utils.py` all exist and are functional code (not stubs)

---

## TASK A: P1 Remaining -- CI Pipeline + Integration Tests

### A1: GitHub Actions CI Pipeline

**File to create:** `.github/workflows/ci.yml`

**Design decisions:**

1. **Runner strategy**: Use `pip install coverage` rather than `uv`. The project has zero external dependencies, so `uv` adds unnecessary complexity in CI. Plain `python` with `coverage` from PyPI is the simplest approach. `coverage` is the only CI dependency.

2. **Matrix**: Python 3.11, 3.12, 3.13 across ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest (9 combinations).

3. **Test discovery**: The existing tests use `sys.path.insert(0, str(SCRIPTS_DIR))` relative to `__file__`, so they work from any working directory. Use `coverage run -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py"` from repo root. This will work because each test file resolves its own import paths.

4. **Coverage reporting**: Run `coverage report --fail-under=80` to enforce the 80% threshold. Also upload coverage as an artifact or use a comment on PRs.

5. **Triggers**: `push` to `master`, `pull_request` to `master`.

**Proposed workflow structure:**

```yaml
name: CI
on:
  push:
    branches: [master]
  pull_request:
    branches: [master]

jobs:
  test:
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        os: [ubuntu-latest, macos-latest, windows-latest]
        python-version: ["3.11", "3.12", "3.13"]
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4
      - uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: ${{ matrix.python-version }}
      - run: pip install coverage
      - run: coverage run -m unittest discover -s tests -p "test_*.py"
      - run: coverage report --fail-under=80
      - run: coverage xml
      - uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        if: matrix.os == 'ubuntu-latest' && matrix.python-version == '3.13'
        with:
          name: coverage-report
          path: coverage.xml
```

**Potential issues:**

- Windows path separator differences: The test files use `Path` throughout, so this should be fine. The `SCRIPTS_DIR = Path(__file__).resolve().parents[1] / "scripts"` pattern works on all platforms.
- The `test_upgrade_checks.py` uses `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location` for the hyphenated `upgrade-memorytree.py`. This works on all platforms.
- Some tests in `test_transcript_utils.py` create temp files with paths containing forward slashes in assertions. These should be fine since they use `Path.as_posix()`.

**Coverage concern:** The `coverage` module only tracks coverage for the test process. Since tests import from `scripts/`, coverage will track those modules. However, the entry-point scripts (`heartbeat.py`, `memorytree_daemon.py`, etc.) are not unit-tested -- their `main()` functions are not called in tests. The 80% threshold should still be achievable since the bulk of logic lives in the internal modules.

### A2: Integration Tests

**File to create:** `tests/test_integration.py`

**Design decisions:**

1. **Import strategy**: Follow the `test_upgrade_checks.py` pattern using `importlib.util.spec_from_file_location()` for hyphenated scripts. For underscore-prefixed modules, use the `sys.path.insert` pattern already established.

2. **Test isolation**: All tests use `tempfile.TemporaryDirectory()` -- no filesystem side effects. For git operations, either mock subprocess calls or create real temporary git repos.

3. **Two test scenarios from TODO.md:**

**Scenario 1: init -> upgrade -> import end-to-end**

```python
class InitUpgradeImportIntegrationTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_full_lifecycle(self):
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            root = Path(tmp)
            # Phase 1: init-memorytree
            # - Call init_main() with --root, --locale en, --goal-summary, --date, --time
            # - Verify: Memory/ dirs created, AGENTS.md exists, goal/todo/chat files exist
            
            # Phase 2: upgrade-memorytree  
            # - Call upgrade_main() with same --root
            # - Verify: detect_state returns "installed", no new files created (preserved)
            
            # Phase 3: Create mock transcript + import
            # - Write a mock claude transcript .jsonl to temp dir
            # - Call import_transcript() with parsed transcript
            # - Verify: Memory/06_transcripts/ populated, clean + raw + manifest exist
            # - Verify: global archive populated
```

Key implementation detail: The `init-memorytree.py` script's `main()` reads `sys.argv` via argparse. For testing, either:
- Import and call the internal functions directly (cleaner)
- Use `importlib.util` to load the module and call `main()` after patching `sys.argv`

The cleaner approach is to test at the function level. The `init-memorytree.py` `main()` is essentially:
```python
create_memory_dirs(root)
paths = resolve_scaffold_paths(root, dt)
scaffold_content_files(paths, templates, goal_summary, project_name, force)
write_template(templates / "agents.md", agents_path, force, {})
```

So the integration test calls these same functions in sequence, then calls `upgrade-memorytree.py`'s `detect_state()` to verify, then calls `import_transcript()`.

**Scenario 2: discover batch scan**

```python
class DiscoverBatchScanIntegrationTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_discover_and_import_multiple(self):
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            # Create mock client directories mimicking ~/.codex/sessions/, 
            # ~/.claude/projects/, ~/.gemini/
            # Place several mock transcript files
            # Patch default_client_roots() to point to temp dirs
            # Call discover_source_files()
            # Verify: all mock files discovered
            # Parse and import each
            # Verify: all appear in global index
```

This requires monkey-patching or injecting test client roots. The `discover_source_files()` function calls `default_client_roots()` which returns hardcoded paths. Options:
- **Option A**: Patch `_transcript_discover.default_client_roots` in the test using `unittest.mock.patch`
- **Option B**: Accept the function signature and pass custom roots through
- **Option C**: The function already accepts `clients` parameter but not custom roots

Option A (mock.patch) is the pragmatic choice since the codebase uses stdlib only and `unittest.mock` is part of stdlib.

**Additional integration tests to consider:**

- **Heartbeat integration**: Create a temp dir with a registered project, mock transcripts, and call `_run_heartbeat()`. Verify transcripts imported and git commands attempted. This requires mocking `subprocess.run` for git operations.
- **Config round-trip**: `save_config()` followed by `load_config()` preserves all fields.
- **Lock mutual exclusion**: `acquire_lock()` returns `True` first time, `False` second time (same process), then `release_lock()` allows re-acquisition.

**Estimated test count:** 8-12 integration tests across the two scenarios.

**What heartbeat.py already does:**

**What memorytree_daemon.py already does:**
- `install`: Detects OS, registers cron (Linux), launchd (macOS), or schtasks (Windows)
- `uninstall`: Removes the scheduled task
- `run-once`: Runs heartbeat.py via subprocess
- `watch`: Continuous loop with configurable interval
- `status`: Shows registration, lock state, config summary
- Uses `uv run python` if available, falls back to `sys.executable`

### P0 Implementation Plan (Phased)

#### Phase 1: Unit Tests for P0 Modules

**File to create:** `tests/test_config_utils.py`

Tests for `_config_utils.py`:
- `load_config()` returns defaults when no file exists
- `load_config()` parses valid TOML correctly (all fields)
- `load_config()` handles invalid TOML gracefully (falls back to defaults)
- `load_config()` handles invalid field values (bad interval, bad log_level, non-bool auto_push)
- `interval_to_seconds()` with various inputs: "5m" -> 300, "300s" -> 300, "1h" -> 3600, invalid -> 300
- `save_config()` produces valid TOML that `load_config()` can round-trip
- `register_project()` adds new project, deduplicates existing project
- `config_path()` returns `~/.memorytree/config.toml`
- `_parse_raw()` validates watch_dirs and projects arrays

**File to create:** `tests/test_lock_utils.py`

Tests for `_lock_utils.py`:
- `acquire_lock()` succeeds on first call
- `acquire_lock()` fails when lock held by current process (simulated by writing PID file)
- `release_lock()` removes lock file
- `acquire_lock()` after `release_lock()` succeeds
- Stale lock detection: write a non-existent PID, then acquire should succeed
- Corrupt lock file (non-numeric content) treated as stale
- `read_lock_pid()` returns PID or None
- `_is_process_alive()` returns True for current PID, False for impossible PID
- Atomic creation via `O_CREAT | O_EXCL` prevents TOCTOU race

Note: Testing `_is_process_alive_windows()` vs `_is_process_alive_unix()` is platform-dependent. The tests should work on all platforms since `_is_process_alive()` dispatches based on `sys.platform`.

**File to create:** `tests/test_alert_utils.py`

Tests for `_alert_utils.py`:
- `write_alert()` creates alerts.json when it does not exist
- `write_alert()` deduplicates by (project, type) key
- `write_alert()` increments count on duplicate
- `read_alerts()` returns empty list when file missing
- `read_alerts()` handles malformed JSON gracefully
- `write_alert_with_threshold()` only writes alert after N consecutive failures
- `reset_failure_count()` clears the counter
- `clear_alerts()` removes the file
- `format_alerts_for_display()` produces readable text
- MAX_ALERTS cap (100) drops oldest

**File to create:** `tests/test_log_utils.py`

Tests for `_log_utils.py`:
- `setup_logging()` returns a configured logger
- `setup_logging()` is idempotent (second call returns same logger, no duplicate handlers)
- `get_logger()` returns the memorytree logger
- `_resolve_level()` maps strings to logging constants
- Log file is created in `~/.memorytree/logs/`

Note: Log tests need care with temp directories. Either patch `_log_file_path()` or accept that tests create files in the real `~/.memorytree/logs/` directory (undesirable). Patching is the right approach.

**File to create:** `tests/test_heartbeat.py`

Tests for `heartbeat.py`:

- `_scan_sensitive()` does not fire on clean text
- `_scan_sensitive()` only writes one alert per transcript

#### Phase 2: Heartbeat Integration Test

This belongs in `tests/test_integration.py` alongside the A2 integration tests.

```python
class HeartbeatIntegrationTest(unittest.TestCase):
    def test_heartbeat_discovers_and_imports(self):
        with tempfile.TemporaryDirectory() as tmp:
            # 1. Create a project dir with Memory/ structure
            # 2. Create a config.toml pointing to this project
            # 3. Create a mock Claude transcript in a temp "~/.claude/projects/" dir
            # 4. Patch default_client_roots and config_path to point to temp dirs
            # 5. Call _run_heartbeat(config)
            # 6. Verify transcript imported into Memory/06_transcripts/
            # 7. Verify _git() was called (mock subprocess)
```

This test requires:
- Patching `_config_utils.config_path` and `_config_utils.memorytree_root`
- Patching `_transcript_discover.default_client_roots`
- Patching `heartbeat._git` to avoid real git operations (or creating a real temp git repo with `git init`)

Creating a real temp git repo is actually simpler and more reliable for integration testing. The sequence: `git init` in temp dir, create Memory/ dirs, run heartbeat with patched paths, verify `git log` shows the commit.

#### Phase 3: First-Install Interactive Setup

This is primarily a documentation/SKILL.md concern. The code infrastructure is already in place:
- `cmd_status()` can check if the daemon is registered
- `register_project()` can add the current project to config
- `save_config()` persists the changes

What needs to happen:
1. In SKILL.md's "On Activation" section, add a check: "If `memorytree-daemon status` shows 'Registered: no', prompt the user to configure and install"
2. The prompting is done by the AI model (text-based, client-agnostic per the cross-client compatibility principle)
3. After user confirms, run `memorytree-daemon install --interval 5m --auto-push true`

This does not require new Python code -- the daemon CLI already supports these flags. It only requires updating SKILL.md to include the detection and prompting logic.

#### Phase 4: Log Rotation Cleanup (Optional Enhancement)

Add a `cleanup_old_logs(max_days: int = 30)` function to `_log_utils.py` that removes log files older than the configured retention period. Call it at the end of each heartbeat run.

This is a minor enhancement and not blocking for P0 completion.

### Verification Steps

**For Task A:**
1. Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
2. Run tests locally with `coverage run -m unittest discover -s tests` to verify coverage percentage
3. Push to a branch and verify GitHub Actions runs successfully on all 9 matrix combinations
4. Verify coverage report shows >= 80% with the new integration tests

**For Task B:**
1. Run the new unit tests: `uv run python tests/test_config_utils.py`, etc.
2. Run `uv run python -m unittest discover -s tests -v` to verify all tests pass (old + new)
3. Run `coverage run -m unittest discover -s tests && coverage report` to check coverage
4. Manual verification: `uv run python scripts/memorytree_daemon.py status` works
5. Manual verification: `uv run python scripts/memorytree_daemon.py run-once` works (in a project with registered config)

### Recommended Execution Order

1. **A1: CI Pipeline** (`.github/workflows/ci.yml`) -- standalone, no code dependencies
2. **B Phase 1: P0 Unit Tests** (`test_config_utils.py`, `test_lock_utils.py`, `test_alert_utils.py`, `test_log_utils.py`, `test_heartbeat.py`) -- adds coverage for existing code
3. **A2 + B Phase 2: Integration Tests** (`test_integration.py`) -- combines both init/upgrade/import and heartbeat integration scenarios
4. **B Phase 3: SKILL.md update** -- documentation change
5. **Update TODO.md** -- mark completed items

Steps 1-3 can be done as separate commits. The CI pipeline (step 1) should be committed first so that subsequent pushes validate the test suite automatically.
